frameworks.name: transcranial direct current stimulation (tDCS) neuromodulation
frameworks.one_line: anodal/cathodal polarity shifts cortical excitability to probe causal role in learning

frameworks.name: visuo-motor coordination learning
frameworks.one_line: visually guided tracking task as the behavioural learning substrate

core_question: Are human MT+/V5 and primary motor cortex causally involved in learning a visually guided tracking task?
key_assumptions: anodal tDCS increases and cathodal tDCS decreases underlying cortical excitability
MT+/V5 mediates visual motion processing relevant to tracking
early-phase tracking accuracy indexes visuo-motor learning
manipulations: stimulation polarity (anodal vs cathodal vs sham)
stimulation site (left MT+/V5 vs contralateral M1 vs primary visual cortex)
learning phase timing during the tracking task
key_findings: anodal tDCS over left V5 increased correct tracking in the early learning phase
anodal tDCS over contralateral M1 also increased correct tracking in the early learning phase
cathodal stimulation produced no significant effect
anodal stimulation of primary visual cortex was not effective for this task
interpretations: MT+/V5 and M1 are causally engaged in early visuo-motor learning, whereas V1 is not
